Manager, Membership Mobilization Remote Full Time Mid Level Share Remote-based in the US Overview GoodPower works to unlock the enormous economic potential of the energy transition—more affordable energy bills, better and more abundant jobs, healthier food, economic security for families and farmers, and a better economy that works for all of us. We operate at the intersection of digital media, smart tech, elections, and renewable energy advocacy. We aim to reach millions of people annually with our work to: lower costs and create jobs, shift culture to transform beliefs and behaviors, and accelerate the deployment of decarbonized technologies. At GoodPower, we are building a team of talented, driven doers who thrive in fast-paced, mission-oriented environments. We value problem solvers who combine strategic thinking with an orientation toward action. We seek collaborative leaders who can inspire and develop others. Our people are eager to innovate, adapt, and scale efforts that can transform the renewable energy economy and strengthen democracy.   Job Summary The Membership Mobilization Manager works to advance GoodPower’s work to accelerate a renewable energy economy through building a membership engagement program across GoodPower and its independent sub-brands who are primed to take high level actions from attending and organizing events in their communities, mobilizing others, calling and writing to elected leaders, submitting public comments, testifying before government bodies, writing op-eds and letters to the editor, engaging in their communities, and sharing their stories.  The Manager spearheads the membership and in-list communications plan, rooted in deep understanding of the various audiences and what most effectively motivates action, how to leverage members to take online and offline actions, and tailors strategies, messages, and action opportunities for each audience to advance GoodPower’s mission and goals.    This is a full time, remote position based anywhere in the United States. The Membership Mobilization Manager will work from home, meet regularly by video chat with GoodPower team members, partners and youth, and conduct activities both online and in-person. The Membership Mobilization Manager must be available for regular travel within the United States to participate in conferences, select actions, events, and to attend staff retreats. This position reports to the Head of Policy and Public Relations.   Key Responsibilities Planning Implementation Leads the launch of a new membership program, by spearheading the planning and execution of GoodPower’s and independent subbrands' membership and in-list communications strategy for regular engagement of audiences through action opportunities, wins and successes, and other interventions. Develops innovative approaches to and implements proven strategies to generate online and offline opportunities.  Collaborates with the National Field Director and State Field Directors in each state to help develop and implement strategic, coordinated organizing plans to engage members around on-the-ground events and activations for issue campaigns to accelerate the renewable energy transition and electoral campaigns, including canvasses, volunteer recruitment, phonebanks and other mobilizations. Collaborates with the National Political Director around key races and opportunities to mobilize voters up and down the ballot. Develop and run the member-to-member electoral field campaign. Collaborates with the Lifecycle Marketing Manager (TBH) to leverage automations, flows and regular communications to move members up a ladder of engagement.  Collaborates with the Data Analytics Director, Research Experimentation and Digital Data teams for deep analysis of the current membership audiences and what messages motivate them to act and build testing and experiments into all communications.  Ensure that all field activities are properly tracked in VAN and metrics and reports are developed for all program activity in collaboration with the Data Analytics Director and National Field Director. Ensure compliance and reporting of all membership related activities working with the Compliance Manager, finance team and legal experts. Collaborates with the Communications Director on an annual and monthly communications calendar with strategic opportunities for intervention across geographies and brands that is regularly updated based on current events, inflection points and other timely engagement opportunities. Develops plan to share wins, successes and stories across membership and in-list audiences to build a sense of self-efficacy. Connects communications with major movement moments, legislative cycles, news headlines, local activation opportunities and other breaking stories to help the organization best position its work. Communication Delivery Writing, styling, programming and sending communications via e-mail, SMS, and other communication channels to GoodPower and sub-brand members and in-list network about action opportunities, GoodPower successes, stories, or other needs as they arise. Conceptualizing and building engaging and strategic actions (e.g. petitions, email-to-target, attending events, etc.) including evergreen actions as well as one-off opportunities that advance members along a ladder of engagement in furtherance of GoodPower’s mission and values. Segmenting lists and tailoring messages to best resonate with each of the appropriate sub-audiences. Ensuring all in-list content aligns with the GoodPower's brand voice and values. Ensuring that communications, field, social media, advertising, and creator marketing groups are advised of any and all member and in-list communications and that plans are maximized with the inclusion of all marketing vehicles. Ensuring welcome flows are timely and relevant to each particular audience. Anticipating and mobilizing to quickly generate communications for rapid response opportunities as they arise. Analysis Collaborate with the Research Experimentation team as well as the Data Analytics Director to identify which messages are most effective at inspiring progressive levels of action. Through independent analysis and in collaboration with the Data team, identify which action opportunities generate the highest level of engagement. Other Duties as Assigned:   Adapt to the evolving needs of a fast-paced organization with flexibility and creativity.   Qualifications Experience 3-5+ years experience in membership engagement, campaigning, organizing, communications, advocacy or similar work, preferably in the political or renewable energy field. Skills Expertise Ability to build a new complex program, collaborating across departments, juggle and think through multiple angles, and ensure message discipline across communications. Prior experience running a successful membership mobilization program. Strong writing skills and ability to adapt tone based on audiences, key messages, and communication vehicles while maintaining message discipline Ability to prioritize multiple, competing opportunities. Experience writing communications (via email programs such as Engaging Networks, Twilio or mobile via MobileCommons). Competency in utilizing CRMs (such as Salesforce) and digital campaigning tools (VAN, Thrutext / Thrutalk, Mobilize and others). Awareness of and ability to communicate the economic impacts,  drivers and motivators of the renewable energy transition. Ability to communicate across audiences from a diversity of demographics and across the political spectrum–whether talking to rural neighbors in Wisconsin or young voters on a college campus in Pennsylvania. Strong analytical skills with ability to set priorities, complete work with minimal supervision, and meet deadlines. Attributes Self-starter with strong organizational skills and creativity. Ability to work independently and as a member of various teams. Demonstrated competence in managing complex projects. Team player who is a strong cross-team collaborator and communicator with an eagerness to jump into other duties than assigned. Proactive, flexible, and solutions-oriented with a commitment to innovation.   Compensation Annual salary range: $78,000-$83,000 commensurate with experience. Generous benefits include: Medical, Dental, Vision, 403b retirement savings plan, Vacation, Sabbatical, Paid Parental Leave, 2 Floating Holidays, 2 Community Service Floating Holidays, sick time, two weeks of full-staff time off (July 4 week and Christmas-New Years week) and 13 observed holidays. GoodPower is an equal-opportunity employer that highly values staff diversity.   This description reflects GoodPower’s assignment of essential functions; it does not restrict the tasks that may be assigned. GoodPower retains the right to change or assign other duties to this position at any time. Employees must be able to perform the essential functions of the position satisfactorily. Reasonable accommodations will be made to enable employees with disabilities to perform the essential functions of their job, absent undue hardship. Collaborates with the National Political Director around key races and opportunities to mobilize voters up and down the ballot. Develop and run the member-to-member electoral field campaign. Collaborates with the Lifecycle Marketing Manager (TBH) to leverage automations, flows and regular communications to move members up a ladder of engagement.  Collaborates with the Data Analytics Director, Research Experimentation and Digital Data teams for deep analysis of the current membership audiences and what messages motivate them to act and build testing and experiments into all communications.  Ensure that all field activities are properly tracked in VAN and metrics and reports are developed for all program activity in collaboration with the Data Analytics Director and National Field Director. Ensure compliance and reporting of all membership related activities working with the Compliance Manager, finance team and legal experts. Collaborates with the Communications Director on an annual and monthly communications calendar with strategic opportunities for intervention across geographies and brands that is regularly updated based on current events, inflection points and other timely engagement opportunities. Develops plan to share wins, successes and stories across membership and in-list audiences to build a sense of self-efficacy. Connects communications with major movement moments, legislative cycles, news headlines, local activation opportunities and other breaking stories to help the organization best position its work. Communication Delivery Writing, styling, programming and sending communications via e-mail, SMS, and other communication channels to GoodPower and sub-brand members and in-list network about action opportunities, GoodPower successes, stories, or other needs as they arise. Conceptualizing and building engaging and strategic actions (e.g. petitions, email-to-target, attending events, etc.) including evergreen actions as well as one-off opportunities that advance members along a ladder of engagement in furtherance of GoodPower’s mission and values. Segmenting lists and tailoring messages to best resonate with each of the appropriate sub-audiences. Ensuring all in-list content aligns with the GoodPower's brand voice and values. Ensuring that communications, field, social media, advertising, and creator marketing groups are advised of any and all member and in-list communications and that plans are maximized with the inclusion of all marketing vehicles. Ensuring welcome flows are timely and relevant to each particular audience. Anticipating and mobilizing to quickly generate communications for rapid response opportunities as they arise. Analysis Collaborate with the Research Experimentation team as well as the Data Analytics Director to identify which messages are most effective at inspiring progressive levels of action. Through independent analysis and in collaboration with the Data team, identify which action opportunities generate the highest level of engagement. Other Duties as Assigned:   Adapt to the evolving needs of a fast-paced organization with flexibility and creativity.   Qualifications Experience 3-5+ years experience in membership engagement, campaigning, organizing, communications, advocacy or similar work, preferably in the political or renewable energy field. Skills Expertise Ability to build a new complex program, collaborating across departments, juggle and think through multiple angles, and ensure message discipline across communications. Prior experience running a successful membership mobilization program. Strong writing skills and ability to adapt tone based on audiences, key messages, and communication vehicles while maintaining message discipline Ability to prioritize multiple, competing opportunities. Experience writing communications (via email programs such as Engaging Networks, Twilio or mobile via MobileCommons). Competency in utilizing CRMs (such as Salesforce) and digital campaigning tools (VAN, Thrutext / Thrutalk, Mobilize and others). Awareness of and ability to communicate the economic impacts,  drivers and motivators of the renewable energy transition. Ability to communicate across audiences from a diversity of demographics and across the political spectrum–whether talking to rural neighbors in Wisconsin or young voters on a college campus in Pennsylvania. Strong analytical skills with ability to set priorities, complete work with minimal supervision, and meet deadlines. Attributes Self-starter with strong organizational skills and creativity. Ability to work independently and as a member of various teams. Demonstrated competence in managing complex projects. Team player who is a strong cross-team collaborator and communicator with an eagerness to jump into other duties than assigned. Proactive, flexible, and solutions-oriented with a commitment to innovation.   Compensation Annual salary range: $78,000-$83,000 commensurate with experience. Generous benefits include: Medical, Dental, Vision, 403b retirement savings plan, Vacation, Sabbatical, Paid Parental Leave, 2 Floating Holidays, 2 Community Service Floating Holidays, sick time, two weeks of full-staff time off (July 4 week and Christmas-New Years week) and 13 observed holidays. GoodPower is an equal-opportunity employer that highly values staff diversity.   This description reflects GoodPower’s assignment of essential functions; it does not restrict the tasks that may be assigned. GoodPower retains the right to change or assign other duties to this position at any time. Employees must be able to perform the essential functions of the position satisfactorily. Reasonable accommodations will be made to enable employees with disabilities to perform the essential functions of their job, absent undue hardship. At GoodPower, we love our jobs, our mission, and what we can do together. GoodPower offers an excellent overall compensation package which includes a competitive base salary commensurate with skills and experience; 95% employer-paid medical, dental, and vision insurance, HSA, and FSA plans; 403b retirement savings plan with matching company contributions; paid vacation, sick leave, floating holidays, volunteer days and 13 paid holidays; monthly internet and wellness stipend; and paid parental leave and sabbatical program. Managing Director Remote Full Time Executive Share Remote-based in the US   Overview GoodPower exists to accelerate the world’s transition to a decarbonized economy—more affordable energy bills, better and more abundant jobs, healthier food, economic security for families and farmers, and a better economy that works for all of us. We operate at the intersection of digital media, smart tech, civic participation, and advocacy to reach millions of people annually with our work to: lower costs and create jobs, shift culture to transform beliefs and behaviors, and accelerate the deployment of decarbonized technologies like: utility-scale renewables, regenerative agriculture and electric vehicles—one individual, one neighborhood, one community at a time.   GoodPower is at an exciting, pivotal moment as we launch our new strategic plan through 2030 to level up all areas of our work–growing our organization 5x over the next five years and relentlessly honing our skills and expertise to be the best that we can be to transform our renewable energy economy.   Job Summary The Managing Director is the CEO’s primary partner and the connective tissue of the organization—ensuring GoodPower operates with clarity, cohesion, and disciplined execution. Acting as an extension of the CEO, this leader drives alignment across teams, strengthens internal systems, and ensures that GoodPower’s priorities translate into measurable impact.   This position is ideal for a mission-driven, highly detail-oriented executive who excels at navigating complexity, anticipating needs, and creating the conditions for the CEO and executive leadership team to perform at their highest level. The right candidate possesses exceptional interpersonal and communication skills, builds trust, motivates teams, and moves work forward with grace and influence.   Key Responsibilities Executive Leadership and Partnership Serve as the CEO’s right hand—supporting planning, decision-making, and organizational priority-setting. Act as a trusted thought partner to the CEO, offering strong judgment, insight, and strategic clarity. Represent the CEO in meetings and decision-making settings when requested, ensuring continuity of leadership and expectations. Work with the Executive Assistant to the CEO to manage the CEO’s bandwidth by ensuring follow-through, clarity of priorities, and proactive problem-solving.   Organizational Alignment and Execution Ensure cross-departmental alignment to keep teams coordinated, informed, and moving in the same direction. Translate goals into clear work plans, timelines, and organizational rhythms that keep leaders accountable. Surface organizational bottlenecks or conflicts early and develop solutions that keep teams aligned and supported. Drive internal communication systems that foster transparency, collaboration, and shared understanding. Be the ultimate stop-gap for programmatic and operational success.   Operational Excellence and Systems Leadership Identify operational challenges and design systems that help GoodPower operate more effectively and scale sustainably. Maintain high-level visibility into program performance, KPIs, and cross-team deliverables; support leaders in using data for decision-making. Partner closely with Operations, HR, and Finance to ensure program and organizational needs are supported with efficiency and compliance.   Leadership Support, Team Enablement and Culture Support coaching of senior program directors, helping them operate with clarity, strategic alignment, and strong execution. Build strong relationships across the organization; serve as a trusted connector who can hold people accountable while fostering respect and collaboration. Champion a culture grounded in communication, accountability, psychological safety, and continuous improvement. Model transparency, diplomacy, calm leadership, and discretion — especially in sensitive organizational conversations.   Board, Funder and Executive Communications Support the CEO in preparing and communicating materials for board meetings, funder briefings, investor conversations, external communications channels and high-stakes moments. Ensure seamless information flow to the CEO and executive team so decisions are grounded in clarity and context. Draft, review, or refine communications, executive updates, and organizational narratives as needed.   Qualifications 5+ years as a Deputy Director, Chief of Staff, Managing Director or CEO for a fast-paced organization spanning operations, program management, and executive support functions. Demonstrated success as a right-hand to a CEO, Executive Director, or C-suite leader. Exceptional interpersonal and communication skills—including the ability to build trust, deliver clear messaging, navigate sensitive dynamics, and influence without authority. Proven track record in organizational alignment, effective execution and managing cross-functional priorities in fast-moving environments. Strong systems thinker with experience designing and improving operational processes at scale. High emotional intelligence, diplomacy, and discretion; excels at managing relationships across levels and functions. Highly proficient in modern collaboration and project management tools (Google Workspace, Slack, Jira or Monday.com, Salesforce), and comfortable with data-driven decision-making. Experience leading or supporting distributed, remote teams is a plus. Thoughtful, steady, proactive, and grounded—able to bring clarity and confidence to ambiguity.   This description reflects GoodPower’s assignment of essential functions; it does not restrict the tasks that may be assigned. GoodPower retains the right to change or assign other duties to this position at any time. Employees must be able to perform the essential functions of the position satisfactorily. Reasonable accommodations will be made to enable employees with disabilities to perform the essential functions of their job, absent undue hardship. GoodPower has a zero-tolerance standard for abuse and inappropriate behavior by staff members.   Job Summary Position Details Annual salary range: $190,000-205,000, commensurate with experience Generous benefits include: Medical, Dental, Vision, 403b retirement savings plan, Vacation, Sabbatical, Paid Parental Leave, 2 Floating Holidays, 2 Community Service Floating Holidays, sick time, two weeks of full-staff time off (July 4 week and Christmas-New Years week) and 13 observed holidays GoodPower is an equal-opportunity employer that highly values staff diversity Location: Remote   Application Guidelines GoodPower is an equal-opportunity employer that highly values diversity, equity, and inclusion and views the climate crisis as a social justice crisis. Founded in 2023, we support local organizations and volunteers working to get clean energy projects built in their communities. In the past two years we have advanced roughly 15 gigawatts of clean power to the grid, approximately 10x the size of the Hoover Dam. POSITION OVERVIEW The Regional Campaign Manager leads Greenlight America's campaigns for clean energy projects across their assigned region. They develop campaign strategies, support partner organizations, cultivate the leadership of volunteers, and track deliverables to ensure that Greenlight America effectively mobilizes grassroots support for clean energy projects. A successful candidate for the position will be a multi-disciplinary campaigner with a blend of skill sets in strategic planning, organizing, communications, lobbying, and coalition management. They will also have a willingness to continually learn new things and approach challenges with a flexible and creative mindset. The position is remote in the assigned region and reports to the Senior Manager, Campaigns. PRIMARY ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Lead campaigns for clean energy projects in the assigned region. Work with partner organizations and local leaders to mobilize volunteers and win siting approvals for clean energy projects, primarily at the county and township level. Develop campaign plans, including goals, strategies, and tactical timelines, in collaboration with partners. Track campaign milestones and seize opportunities. Provide support to partner organizations and volunteer leaders. Conduct regular check-in calls, host training sessions, and create advocacy materials. Work with other campaign staff on shared resources for Greenlight America's campaign hub model. Build relationships with organizations in the assigned region, including but not limited to environmental, labor, faith, farming, social justice, and business groups, in collaboration with Greenlight America's partnerships team. Oversee grant agreements with partner organizations and ensure deliverables are met. Engage in regular reporting of campaign data. Work with consultants and manage campaign budgets. May occasionally take on campaigns outside of the assigned region. EXPERIENCE AND QUALIFICATIONS We are looking for candidates who have the following qualifications: 3+ years of relevant experience. Strong candidates for this position may have a background in grassroots organizing, issue advocacy, community engagement, political campaigns, coalition management, or similar. Successful track record of achieving ambitious campaign outcomes. Experience at the local level is a strong plus. Strong project management skills and attention to detail. Experience working with partner organizations and volunteers. Willingness to meet people where they are and build alliances across the political spectrum. Excellent communications skills, both verbal and written, and comfort talking to people at all levels of expertise and seniority. Experience with digital campaigning tools and tactics. Willingness to think creatively, take risks, objectively evaluate results, and iterate in a fast-paced environment. Passion for accelerating the clean energy transition. COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS The base annual salary range for this position is $75,000-$100,000. We offer an excellent benefits package which includes 100% employer paid premiums for medical, dental, and vision insurance, unlimited paid time off, monthly technology stipend, and retirement contributions through 401k and Roth plans. TO APPLY Submit a resume and respond to the applicant questionnaire via this link . ​ Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the role is filled. If you require accommodations so that you may participate in the selection process, please email accommodations(@)greenlightamerica.org . ​ Due to the volume of applicants, we will only contact applicants whose skill sets, and experience meet our needs. Apply Now Apply Now Regional Campaign Manager Campaigns Department Remote in AR, IA, IL, KS, LA, MI, MN, MO, OK, TX, and WI with occasional travel Full Time $75,000 to $100,000 Apply Now Diversity and Equity Opportunity Greenlight America is proud to be an equal opportunity employer, and an anti-racist organization that prohibits discrimination in its governance, programs and activities on the basis of race, color, national origin, age, disability, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, genetic information, reprisal, marital status, amnesty, status as a covered veteran, because all or part of an individual's income is derived from public assistance or for any other non-merit based factor. Women, people of color, LGBTQ+ people, and members of other historically and presently disenfranchised populations are strongly encouraged to apply. Our goal is for a work experience that has meaning and impact on the world and on our professional lives, and a workplace where you can feel supported and proud. You can learn more about us at greenlightamerica.org . ​ This position also supports Greenlight Action, an affiliated 501(c)(4) organization. We do this by partnering with local groups and activists to run strategic campaigns that win approval for clean energy projects in their communities. Our founding team has led some of the largest grassroots organizing networks in the country, and we are building a scalable model for engaging and mobilizing local residents in support of clean energy. POSITION OVERVIEW The People Operations Manager will play a crucial role managing and running the systems that power our employee experience. This role is responsible for day-to-day HR and operational tasks, ensuring smooth execution of HR processes and supporting staff across the employee lifecycle. ​ A successful candidate for this position will have excellent problem-solving skills, the ability to think strategically, and a hands-on approach to managing day-to-day human resources. They will be adept at juggling multiple priorities and will possess a strong sense of initiative. The ideal candidate will have experience working in a nonprofit setting, a deep commitment to the mission of accelerating the clean energy transition, and a track record of successfully supporting organizational growth and development. They should be highly organized, detail-oriented, and comfortable working both independently and as part of a team. ​ The position is remote and reports to the Senior People Operations Manager. PRIMARY ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES People Operations Serve as a trusted advisor to staff on HR matters, including benefits, policies, and employee questions. Continuously enhance HR systems and administrative processes to improve efficiency and coordination between functions, such as HRIS optimization, workflow enhancements, and data cleanup. Ensure accuracy of HRIS system(s) and maintain up-to-date employee records and documentation. Maintain HR documentation, employee handbooks, and SOP (standard operating procedures) guides. Support benefits administration with our PEO and ensure compliance with federal and multi-state employment regulations. Partner with the Senior People Operations Manager to execute the performance review cycle, including scheduling, tracking, and reporting on completion and outcomes. Support the Senior People Operations Manager in executing compensation processes, including tracking merit increases and promotions. Assist in administering payroll-related data for accuracy. Track PTO and leave requests. Maintain knowledge of emerging trends, best practices, and regulatory updates in human resources to ensure compliance and continuous improvement. Support the Senior People Operations Manager on special projects as needed. ​ ​ Recruiting Talent Acquisition Manage full-cycle hiring logistics in partnership with hiring managers. Lead onboarding and offboarding processes to ensure smooth transitions. ​ ​ Training Development Develop and manage an internship program. Track and ensure completion of HR training and professional development activities. Support with scheduling and administering staff training programs. Support in designing and delivering staff training logistics, materials, and reporting. Collect feedback on training and provide reporting to the Senior People Operations Manager. Culture Engagement Support execution of staff retreats, engagement events, and recognition programs. Support employee engagement survey administration, including reminders, collection, and basic reporting. Collaborate with managers and the Senior People Operations Manager to identify opportunities to strengthen culture and engagement. EXPERIENCE AND QUALIFICATIONS We are especially interested in candidates with the following qualifications: ​ 3+ years experience of relevant professional experience in human resources or people operations Experience with multi-state employment compliance Ability to analyze and resolve complex issues Ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously Passion for accelerating the clean energy transition Preferred: experience in California human resources practices COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS The base annual salary for this position is $75,000-$100,000. We offer an excellent benefits package which includes 100% employer paid premiums for medical, dental, and vision insurance, unlimited paid time off, monthly technology stipend, and retirement contributions through 401k and Roth plans. TO APPLY Please apply at this link by submitting a resume and cover letter. Priority applicants will be considered through April 17, 2026. If you require accommodations so that you may participate in the selection process, please email accommodations (@) greenlightamerica.org . ​ Due to the volume of applicants, we will only contact applicants whose skill sets, and experience meet our needs. Founded in 2023, we support local organizations and volunteers working to get clean energy projects built in their communities. In the past two years we have advanced roughly 15 gigawatts of clean power to the grid, approximately 10x the size of the Hoover Dam. POSITION OVERVIEW The Regional Campaign Manager leads Greenlight America's campaigns for clean energy projects across their assigned region. They develop campaign strategies, support partner organizations, cultivate the leadership of volunteers, and track deliverables to ensure that Greenlight America effectively mobilizes grassroots support for clean energy projects. A successful candidate for the position will be a multi-disciplinary campaigner with a blend of skill sets in strategic planning, organizing, communications, lobbying, and coalition management. They will also have a willingness to continually learn new things and approach challenges with a flexible and creative mindset. The position is remote in the assigned region and reports to the Senior Manager, Campaigns. PRIMARY ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Lead campaigns for clean energy projects in the assigned region. Work with partner organizations and local leaders to mobilize volunteers and win siting approvals for clean energy projects, primarily at the county and township level. Develop campaign plans, including goals, strategies, and tactical timelines, in collaboration with partners. Track campaign milestones and seize opportunities. Provide support to partner organizations and volunteer leaders. Conduct regular check-in calls, host training sessions, and create advocacy materials. Work with other campaign staff on shared resources for Greenlight America's campaign hub model. Build relationships with organizations in the assigned region, including but not limited to environmental, labor, faith, farming, social justice, and business groups, in collaboration with Greenlight America's partnerships team. Oversee grant agreements with partner organizations and ensure deliverables are met. Engage in regular reporting of campaign data. Work with consultants and manage campaign budgets. May occasionally take on campaigns outside of the assigned region. EXPERIENCE AND QUALIFICATIONS We are looking for candidates who have the following qualifications: 3+ years of relevant experience. Strong candidates for this position may have a background in grassroots organizing, issue advocacy, community engagement, political campaigns, coalition management, or similar. Successful track record of achieving ambitious campaign outcomes. Experience at the local level is a strong plus. Strong project management skills and attention to detail. Experience working with partner organizations and volunteers. Willingness to meet people where they are and build alliances across the political spectrum. Excellent communications skills, both verbal and written, and comfort talking to people at all levels of expertise and seniority. Experience with digital campaigning tools and tactics. Willingness to think creatively, take risks, objectively evaluate results, and iterate in a fast-paced environment. Passion for accelerating the clean energy transition. COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS The base annual salary range for this position is $75,000-$100,000. We offer an excellent benefits package which includes 100% employer paid premiums for medical, dental, and vision insurance, unlimited paid time off, monthly technology stipend, and retirement contributions through 401k and Roth plans. TO APPLY Submit a resume and respond to the applicant questionnaire via this link . ​ Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the role is filled. If you require accommodations so that you may participate in the selection process, please email accommodations(@)greenlightamerica.org . ​ Due to the volume of applicants, we will only contact applicants whose skill sets, and experience meet our needs. We do this by partnering with local groups and activists to run strategic campaigns that win approval for clean energy projects in their communities. Our founding team has led some of the largest grassroots organizing networks in the country, and we are building a scalable model for engaging and mobilizing local residents in support of clean energy. POSITION OVERVIEW The People Operations Manager will play a crucial role managing and running the systems that power our employee experience. This role is responsible for day-to-day HR and operational tasks, ensuring smooth execution of HR processes and supporting staff across the employee lifecycle. ​ A successful candidate for this position will have excellent problem-solving skills, the ability to think strategically, and a hands-on approach to managing day-to-day human resources. They will be adept at juggling multiple priorities and will possess a strong sense of initiative. The ideal candidate will have experience working in a nonprofit setting, a deep commitment to the mission of accelerating the clean energy transition, and a track record of successfully supporting organizational growth and development. They should be highly organized, detail-oriented, and comfortable working both independently and as part of a team. ​ The position is remote and reports to the Senior People Operations Manager. PRIMARY ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES People Operations Serve as a trusted advisor to staff on HR matters, including benefits, policies, and employee questions. Continuously enhance HR systems and administrative processes to improve efficiency and coordination between functions, such as HRIS optimization, workflow enhancements, and data cleanup. Ensure accuracy of HRIS system(s) and maintain up-to-date employee records and documentation. Maintain HR documentation, employee handbooks, and SOP (standard operating procedures) guides. Support benefits administration with our PEO and ensure compliance with federal and multi-state employment regulations. Partner with the Senior People Operations Manager to execute the performance review cycle, including scheduling, tracking, and reporting on completion and outcomes. Support the Senior People Operations Manager in executing compensation processes, including tracking merit increases and promotions. Assist in administering payroll-related data for accuracy. Track PTO and leave requests. Maintain knowledge of emerging trends, best practices, and regulatory updates in human resources to ensure compliance and continuous improvement. Support the Senior People Operations Manager on special projects as needed. ​ ​ Recruiting Talent Acquisition Manage full-cycle hiring logistics in partnership with hiring managers. Lead onboarding and offboarding processes to ensure smooth transitions. ​ ​ Training Development Develop and manage an internship program. Track and ensure completion of HR training and professional development activities. Support with scheduling and administering staff training programs. Support in designing and delivering staff training logistics, materials, and reporting. Collect feedback on training and provide reporting to the Senior People Operations Manager. Culture Engagement Support execution of staff retreats, engagement events, and recognition programs. Support employee engagement survey administration, including reminders, collection, and basic reporting. Collaborate with managers and the Senior People Operations Manager to identify opportunities to strengthen culture and engagement. EXPERIENCE AND QUALIFICATIONS We are especially interested in candidates with the following qualifications: ​ 3+ years experience of relevant professional experience in human resources or people operations Experience with multi-state employment compliance Ability to analyze and resolve complex issues Ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously Passion for accelerating the clean energy transition Preferred: experience in California human resources practices COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS The base annual salary for this position is $75,000-$100,000. We offer an excellent benefits package which includes 100% employer paid premiums for medical, dental, and vision insurance, unlimited paid time off, monthly technology stipend, and retirement contributions through 401k and Roth plans. TO APPLY Please apply at this link by submitting a resume and cover letter. Priority applicants will be considered through April 17, 2026. Posted on April 29, Full Time Litigation Northeast Office

If you need reasonable accommodation in order to complete the application, interview, or some other portion of the selection process for a job at Earthjustice, please contact us at jobs@earthjustice.org.

At this time, new applications are no longer being accepted for this position.

  • If you have already applied but have not received an update yet, you remain under consideration for this role.
  • All applicants will hear from our team, whether to schedule an interview or to let you know we are not moving forward with your candidacy for this particular opportunity. Thank you for your patience.

For additional career opportunities at Earthjustice, please see all of our open job positions. We have many offices and hiring teams nationwide, and Earthjustice is growing.

We appreciate your interest in Earthjustice.

At Arevon, you will have the opportunity to pursue your career passions alongside the industry’s best and brightest talent and work collaboratively to foster a more sustainable world. Join our highly talented and diverse team. Explore Open Positions Work With Us We are not only passionate about renewable energy, we are also dedicated to fostering a culture where innovation thrives, diversity is celebrated, and every voice matters. We respect diverse perspectives, beliefs, and values, and we are committed to our talented team. Between now and the end of the, we are leveling up all areas of our work, and plan to grow our organization by 5x to meet this critical moment with boldness and excellence. We are seeking talented, professional, creative and highly skilled teammates who are are energized by a dynamic, fast-paced work environment of continuous growth. At GoodPower, we love our jobs, our mission, and what we can do together. GoodPower offers an excellent overall compensation package which includes a competitive base salary commensurate with skills and experience; 95% employer-paid medical, dental, and vision insurance, HSA, and FSA plans;retirement savings plan with matching company contributions; paid vacation, sick leave, floating holidays, volunteer days and 13 paid holidays; monthly internet and wellness stipend; and paid parental leave and sabbatical program. GoodPower is an equal opportunity employer that deeply values diversity, equity, and inclusion. We strongly encourage applications from people of color, Indigenous people, individuals who experience gender oppression, people with disabilities, and members of the LGBTQIA+ community. Lifecycle Marketing Manager Remote Full Time Mid Level Share Remote-based in the US   Overview GoodPower works to unlock the enormous economic potential of the energy transition—more affordable energy bills, better and more abundant jobs, healthier food, economic security for families and farmers, and a better economy that works for all of us. We operate at the intersection of digital media, smart tech, elections, and renewable energy advocacy. We aim to reach millions of people annually with our work to: lower costs and create jobs, shift culture to transform beliefs and behaviors, and accelerate the deployment of decarbonized technologies. At GoodPower, we are building a team of talented, driven doers who thrive in fast-paced, mission-oriented environments. We value problem solvers who combine strategic thinking with an orientation toward action. We seek collaborative leaders who can inspire and develop others. Our people are eager to innovate, adapt, and scale efforts that can transform the renewable energy economy and strengthen democracy.   Job Summary GoodPower  is seeking a data-driven and impact-oriented Lifecycle Marketing Manager to manage our supporter journey strategy. This role will design, execute, and optimize cross-channel lifecycle programs that move users from awareness to Members — and from one-time participants to lifelong climate champions. You will be responsible for architecting tracking full-funnel, multi-touch journeys across email, SMS, Messenger, social ads, and emerging channels — powered by GoodPower’s growing first-party data infrastructure. You’ll partner closely with Membership Mobilization Manager, creative, digital ads, and data science teams to create high-performing, personalized experiences that drive engagement, advocacy, donations, and retention. This is a full time, remote position based anywhere in the United States. The Membership Mobilization Manager will work from home, meet regularly by video chat with GoodPower team members, partners and youth, and conduct activities both online and in-person. The Membership Mobilization Manager must be available for regular travel within the United States to participate in conferences, select actions, events, and to attend staff retreats. This position reports to the Sr. Director of Digital Marketing.    Key Responsibilities Develop and execute GoodPower’s supporter lifecycle strategy across digital touchpoints, including onboarding, engagement, advocacy mobilization, reactivation, and retention. Design and build cross-channel journeys in platforms like Engaging Networks, ManyChat, Klaviyo, and n8n. Leverage GoodPower’s robust BigQuery user data to build actionable segments, cohorts, and triggers. Partner with campaign and creative teams to deliver message-matched content based on supporter actions, demographics, and climate interests. Implement A/B and multivariate testing to optimize messaging, frequency, and channel mix. Monitor and report on lifecycle KPIs: open/click rates, conversion, opt-out, reactivation, and ROI. Collaborate with the data team to define and refine lifecycle stages (e.g., New, Active, At-Risk, Lapsed). Build re-engagement and win-back strategies for inactive supporters. Contribute to GoodPower’s CRM/CDP roadmap with automation ideas and personalization strategies.   Qualifications Required Qualifications 5+ years in lifecycle marketing, CRM, or engagement marketing, ideally at a nonprofit, mission-driven org, or growth-oriented company. Deep experience with email marketing platforms, marketing automation tools, and CRM systems (e.g., Engaging Networks, Klaviyo, n8n, HubSpot, Salesforce). Proven success designing journeys that drive conversions, retention, or re-engagement. Strong analytical skills — comfortable analyzing funnel metrics and using SQL or data dashboards. Experience with audience segmentation, personalization, and message testing. Passion for youth-led climate action and social justice is a strong plus. Preferred Qualifications Familiarity with CDPs, journey orchestration tools, or open-source automation platforms like n8n. Knowledge of Meta ad flows (e.g., lead gen, Messenger bots) and their integration with CRM systems. Experience with marketing automation tools Experience working in cross-functional teams and agile environments. Being bilingual (especially Spanish) is a plus.   Compensation Annual salary range: $78,000-$83,000 commensurate with experience. Generous benefits include: Medical, Dental, Vision,retirement savings plan, Vacation, Sabbatical, Paid Parental Leave, 2 Floating Holidays, 2 Community Service Floating Holidays, sick time, two weeks of full-staff time off (July 4 week and Christmas-New Years week) and 13 observed holidays. GoodPower is an equal-opportunity employer that highly values staff diversity.   This description reflects GoodPower’s assignment of essential functions; it does not restrict the tasks that may be assigned. GoodPower retains the right to change or assign other duties to this position at any time. Employees must be able to perform the essential functions of the position satisfactorily. Reasonable accommodations will be made to enable employees with disabilities to perform the essential functions of their job, absent undue hardship. GoodPower has a zero-tolerance standard for abuse and inappropriate behavior by staff members. As part of Octopus Energy Group, we’re creating an innovative approach to trading that will accelerate the transition to a Net Zero world. With the growth of renewables and a push toward decarbonising heating and transport, greater flexibility in the grid is essential. We are building cutting-edge technology to optimise everything, from domestic EV charging to grid-scale batteries, to meet the global demand for energy flexibility. We’re looking for passionate and unconventional thinkers to join us on this journey, bringing a diversity of experience and ideas to shape a more efficient, flexible, and sustainable energy system. With an increase in variable and distributed supply, using data for trading and forecasting has never been more important. Octopus has always had a tech-first approach, and our trading and analytics tools are all built in-house by specialised domain teams, focussed on areas like grid-scale batteries or market fundamentals, all building on a common platform that is managed by our core engineering team. We are seeking a software engineer to join the core engineering team and help own the developer experience. The Foundation Board of Directors established the following goals to guide the Foundation’s work: Solve the climate crisis primarily through a successful transition to a resource-efficient, clean energy economy that better serves people and nature; Secure protections for public lands and waters, promote healthy ecosystems and communities, and fight for clean air and water; Expand opportunities for more people to explore, enjoy, and protect the planet by supporting programs and policies that reach across economic, cultural, and community lines to get people outdoors; and Build a diverse, inclusive environmental movement that reflects and represents today’s American public; and prioritizes important connections between environmental health and social justice. Position Summary: The Staff Accountant will play a pivotal role in maintaining accurate financial records, processing transactions, and assisting with audits to support the Foundation’s mission. We strongly encourage candidates to check out our website at [www.renewhome.com]( to about the world-changing work we are doing. **Role Summary** You'll lead the Infrastructure team — a group of 5-8 engineers responsible for the foundation everything else at Renew Home runs on. The team owns cloud accounts, networking, security, CI/CD, deployment tooling, observability, and the developer experience that surrounds all of it. Other engineering teams build on top of what your team provides — your team's leverage shows up in how fast the rest of the company can ship. This is a hands-on engineering manager role. You'll spend most of your time on people, planning, and cross-team alignment, but you should be technical enough to debate architecture, review a tricky design doc, and sense when a project is in trouble before it lands on your desk. **What You Will Do** - The team. Hiring, growth, performance, and career development for seven engineers across three sub-disciplines. Setting expectations, giving direct feedback, and building a team people want to stay on. - The roadmap. Translating company priorities into an infra roadmap that balances reliability work, platform investment, security posture, and the steady stream of \"unblock the product team\" asks. - Operational health. Participate in on-call rotations, On-call rotation quality, incident response, postmortem culture, and the SLOs to keep the platform healthy. - Cross-team partnership. You'll be the primary point of contact for product engineering, data, and security leadership. Your team's reputation lives or dies by how well it serves its consumers. - Security posture. Partnering with security engineering to drive identity, access, audit, and compliance work — making sure security is built into the platform rather than bolted on. - CI/CD and test automation. The health of the pipelines, runners, and test infrastructure the entire engineering org relies on. This role involves setting strategies, following developments in the stakeholder process, building relationships with RTO staff and other stakeholders, and advocating for reforms to remove barriers and expand opportunities for advanced energy technologies to compete in these markets.

The Director will report to the Managing Director – Wholesale Markets, and will supervise one Senior Principal supporting United’s work in MISO/SPP. The Director will be part of United’s Expanding Wholesale Markets team, and will also work closely with United’s state regulatory and legislative staff.

This includes internal and external communications, coordinating the Climate Action Fellowship program, organizing events across campus, as well as performing administrative tasks for overall programs. This is a full-time, benefits eligible, hourly position with a hiring range of $21.27 - $25.42 per hour. This position is gift funded for one year with the possibility of an extension. We Are** Renew Home is on a mission to change how we power the world by making it easier for customers to save energy and money at home as part of the largest residential virtual power plant in North America. We partner with industry-leading brands to better manage residential energy for users by prioritizing efficiency, savings, and comfort — and cleaner energy for everyone. We are an. We strongly encourage candidates to check out our website at [www.renewhome.com]( to about the world-changing work we are doing. **Role Summary** Renew Home is hiring a Senior Technical Program Manager to own the planning, execution, and operational health of complex, cross-functional programs across our VPP platform and consumer energy products. This role sits within the Technical Program Management team, reporting to the Director of Technical Program Management, and operates with significant independence to define how programs are structured, tracked, and delivered. This is not a coordination role. The TPM at this level is expected to build and own the frameworks that make execution reliable, communicate tradeoffs and risks in ways that drive decisions, and serve as an operational and technical thought partner to Engineering and Product leadership. Significant cross-functional situational awareness is required -- this person will regularly work across Engineering, Product, Design, Business Development, Customer Success, Grid Operations, and strategic planning functions. **What You Will Do** - Lead -end delivery of complex, multi-team programs spanning Engineering, Product, and Design, from scoping through launch and operational handoff. - Define and maintain the planning frameworks, milestone structures, and risk models that enable predictable delivery across your programs -- not just track against plans others set. - Identify, escalate, and drive resolution of cross-functional dependencies, blockers, and tradeoffs with clear recommendations, not just status updates. - Partner with Engineering and Product leadership to set and pressure-test roadmap sequencing, resource assumptions, and launch readiness criteria. - Drive programs through the full product lifecycle, with particular focus on launch readiness, post-launch metric validation, operational handoff to sustaining teams, and ensuring downstream stakeholders -- including Business Development, Customer Success, Grid Operations, and partner-facing functions -- are informed, equipped, and ready to operate before a program is formally closed. - Develop and share operational practices and retrospective learnings that raise the execution standard across the broader TPM team. - Serve as a decision-support resource for your stakeholders -- synthesizing technical and schedule complexity into clear options and tradeoffs for leadership. - Maintain cross-functional situational awareness across interdependent programs, surfacing risks and misalignments before they become escalations. **Who We Are** Renew Home is on a mission to change how we power the world by making it easier for customers to save energy and money at home as part of the largest residential virtual power plant in North America.
